RT by @lookner: The greatest challenge now is no longer military pressure, it's escalation management. It is becoming increasingly clear that events are beginning to slip beyond anyone's control. With virtually no trust between Washington and Tehran, mediators who appear increasingly exhausted, and a dialogue conducted primarily through military strikes, the risk of further escalation is now higher than the prospects for de-escalation. That is unlikely to change unless one side makes a meaningful concession, and there is little evidence that Tehran is prepared to do so. This dynamic has been reinforced by recent developments inside Iran. The combination of President Trump's approach and the regime's demonstration of strength during Ali Khamenei's funeral appears to have strengthened the conviction among key figures in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps that they not only enjoy sufficient domestic backing, but also have little choice but to intensify their response to continued U.S. military operations. Under these conditions, managing the escalation spiral becomes extraordinarily difficult. Each military action establishes a new deterrence equation, prompting the other side to respond in kind. Although both Washington and Tehran initially appeared interested in keeping the confrontation within certain limits, the conflict is now moving alarmingly close to a point where those limits could collapse. If that happens, the confrontation could expand well beyond its current scope, drawing Gulf states more directly into the conflict and placing additional strategic targets and critical infrastructure at risk. There are no effective guardrails, the mediators are losing leverage, one side believes additional pressure will eventually weaken Iran enough to force concessions, while the other increasingly views this as an existential struggle. Unless something significant changes soon, this conflict is on a trajectory toward a far more dangerous phase, one that may become increasingly difficult for either side to contain. #iran

Americans abroad should follow the guidance in security alerts issued by the nearest U.S. embassy or consulate. Flight cancellations and periodic airspace closures may cause travel disruptions. U.S. diplomatic facilities, including outside the Middle East, have been targeted. Groups supportive of Iran may target other U.S. interests overseas or locations associated with the United States and/or Americans throughout the world. Please read carefully our Travel Advisory, country information, and any recent security alerts when planning travel at http://travel.state.gov/destination. Iran 🇮🇷 has expanding its target base this week, reportedly attacking Jordan 🇯🇴, Syria 🇸🇾, Saudi Arabia 🇸🇦, Qatar 🇶🇦, Oman 🇴🇲, and the UAE 🇦🇪 in addition to Kuwait 🇰🇼, Bahrain 🇧🇭, and Iraq 🇮🇶, which have taken the brunt of Iran's recent attacks.
